items.headline: LeBron James Sets New NBA Record for Games Played!
items.summary: LeBron James made history last night, playing in his 1,612th regular-season game, surpassing Robert Parish for the all-time record. The milestone occurred in the Lakers' thrilling 105-104 comeback victory over the Magic, sealed by a last-second three-pointer from Luke Kennard.

items.headline: Kevin Durant Climbs to 5th on NBA All-Time Scoring List
items.summary: Kevin Durant etched his name further into the history books, passing Michael Jordan for 5th on the NBA's career scoring list. Durant scored 27 points in the Rockets' narrow 123-122 win against the Heat.

items.headline: Luka Doncic Hits 16th Technical Foul, Faces Potential Suspension
items.summary: Lakers star Luka Doncic, despite scoring 33 points in their win over the Magic, was assessed his 16th technical foul of the season. This automatically triggers a one-game suspension if not rescinded by the league.

items.headline: Lakers Edge Magic on Kennard's Buzzer-Beater
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers secured a dramatic 105-104 victory over the Orlando Magic, thanks to Luke Kennard's game-winning three with 0.6 seconds left.

items.headline: Gilgeous-Alexander's 40 Powers Thunder Past Wizards
items.summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander exploded for 40 points, leading the Oklahoma City Thunder to a 132-111 win over the Washington Wizards, extending their winning streak to 11 games.

items.headline: Hawks Soar Past Warriors Behind Daniels' Season-High
items.summary: Dyson Daniels scored a season-high 28 points as the Atlanta Hawks defeated the Golden State Warriors 126-110.

items.headline: Bucks Hand Suns Fifth Straight Loss
items.summary: Ryan Rollins' 26 points helped the Milwaukee Bucks snap a losing streak with a 108-105 victory over the Phoenix Suns, who have now lost five in a row.

items.headline: Rockets Edge Heat in Durant's Record Night
items.summary: The Houston Rockets secured a tight 123-122 win against the Miami Heat on a night where Kevin Durant made history.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: 40 Points
items.statContext: OKC's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander led all scorers with 40 points against the Wizards, extending his record for consecutive 20-point road games to 63.

items.headline: Luka Doncic: 33 Points
items.statContext: Luka Doncic powered the Lakers' offense with 33 points in their thrilling win over the Magic.

items.headline: Austin Reaves: 26 Points
items.statContext: Austin Reaves contributed 26 points for the Lakers in their comeback victory against Orlando.

items.headline: Ryan Rollins: 26 Points, 10 Rebounds
items.statContext: Milwaukee's Ryan Rollins had a strong outing with 26 points and 10 rebounds in the win over Phoenix.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Flirts with Triple-Double (Friday)
items.summary: Israeli forward Deni Avdija had a stellar performance on Friday night, posting 25 points, 8 rebounds, and 5 assists in the Portland Trail Blazers' 108-104 victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves.
items.statContext: Deni Avdija (POR) continues a dynamic stretch, averaging 24.2 points, 6.6 assists, and 7.0 rebounds per game this season.

items.headline: Dyson Daniels: Season-High 28 Points
items.statContext: Dyson Daniels exploded for a season-high 28 points to lead the Hawks over the Warriors.

items.headline: Isaiah Hartenstein: Near Triple-Double
items.statContext: OKC's Isaiah Hartenstein filled the stat sheet with 20 rebounds and 10 assists, nearly achieving a triple-double (9 points) against the Wizards.

items.headline: Rookie Standout: Cooper Flagg Leads in Scoring
items.statContext: Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg currently leads all first-year players in points per game with an impressive 20.1 average.

items.headline: Injury Report: Updates on Key Players Across the NBA
items.summary: The latest injury report for Sunday, March 22, indicates Minnesota's Naz Reid is questionable (ankle) and Portland's Jerami Grant is questionable (foot). Boston's Jaylen Brown is probable (quad). Notable absences include Dillon Brooks (PHX) with a fractured left hand, Joel Embiid (PHI) with an oblique strain, and Tyrese Maxey (PHI) with a finger injury. Kyle Kuzma (MIL) also left last night's game with Achilles tightness.

items.headline: Utah Jazz Sign Kennedy Chandler to 10-Day Contract
items.summary: The Utah Jazz announced on March 21, 2026, they have signed guard Kennedy Chandler to a 10-day contract. Chandler has been averaging 17.0 points and 9.0 assists in the G League this season.

items.headline: Playoff Picture Heating Up: Standings Update
items.summary: As the regular season winds down, the playoff race intensifies. The Detroit Pistons lead the Eastern Conference with a 51-19 record, while the Oklahoma City Thunder hold the top spot in the West at 55-15. The Los Angeles Lakers have improved their standing, now 3rd in the West with a 46-25 record after their ninth consecutive win.

items.headline: Trail Blazers vs. Nuggets
items.summary: Deni Avdija and the Trail Blazers will face Nikola Jokic and the Nuggets in Denver tonight.
